message:
chore: absorb concurrent io_uring struct additions + close bd-j946

Closes bd-j946 and files bd-1isd for minor vDSO defensive-handling
follow-ups spotted during review.

Absorbs concurrent syscall struct additions for io_uring
(IoUringSqringOffsets / IoUringCqringOffsets) and veneer tests made
by another swarm agent. cargo check --workspace is clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id": "bd-1hq", "title": "Kernel: Groebner normal form for violation signatures (design)", "description": "Design Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization for anomaly/violation signatures.

Goal:
- Produce stable root-cause IDs from many overlapping anomaly signals.
- Avoid double-counting and improve sparse latent-cause recovery.

Design tasks:
- Define signature variables (small integer features derived from cached states).
- Define constraints/relations and what the normal form represents.
- Choose offline artifact: Groebner basis + reduction table (no heavy algebra at runtime).

Acceptance criteria:
- Concrete signature schema + deterministic reduction procedure + output ID format.", "notes": "Drafted design doc: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/grobner_normal_form_design.md (v1 atom schema A00..A24 + optional class vars C0..C5; relations/term order; offline artifact format; deterministic reduction; id format).", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "BrightCave", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:32:55.351807261Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-10T19:13:26.094075093Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-10T19:13:26.094056689Z", "close_reason": "Drafted crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/grobner_normal_form_design.md with v1 schema, relations/term order, offline artifact sketch, deterministic reduction procedure, and gn1 id format.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

Rewrote history in temp clone via git-filter-repo, enforced LICENSE presence in all commits with commit-callback, force-pushed main/master, verified all 295 commits now contain LICENSE and only rider blob remains; releases/tags are empty.", "created_at": "2026-02-19T01:47:55Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-1ik", "title": "Opportunity matrix workflow: score hotspots and enforce score>=2.0 before implementation", "description": "Extreme optimization mapping.

Deliverables:
- Implement hotspot scoring matrix with impact/confidence/effort and computed score.
- Gate optimization work so only score>=2.0 items proceed.

Acceptance:
- Optimization backlog always includes explicit score rationale.
- Low-score work remains deferred unless manually justified.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "notes": "2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): opportunity_matrix_test (7 pass) + gate script. Scoring dimensions defined, threshold enforced (>=2.0), scores match formula, entries have required fields.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CrimsonCove", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:48:12.172285695Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z", "close_reason": "Opportunity matrix workflow operational. 7 harness tests pass. check_opportunity_matrix.sh gate validates scores, thresholds, and formula consistency.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "perf", "planning"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-1ik", "depends_on_id": "bd-26o", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1isd","title":"vDSO fastpath: defensive handling for positive rc + cross-arch version strings","description":"Review of time_abi.rs vDSO fastpath (commit 92989d2) flagged two minor issues that don't affect current correctness:

1) In raw_clock_gettime (line 102-105), if vdso_clock_gettime returns an unexpected POSITIVE rc (buggy vDSO or future ABI change), the current code computes set_abi_errno(-rc) which yields a negative errno value. Per POSIX errno must be positive. Fix: guard with rc < 0 check before negating, or treat any non-{0, -ENOSYS} result as a kernel anomaly warranting fallback.

2) resolve_vdso_symbols (line 166, 178) hardcodes c"LINUX_2.6" as the dlvsym version. x86_64's vDSO uses LINUX_2.6, but aarch64 uses LINUX_2.6.39 and riscv64 uses LINUX_4.15. Currently falls back to dlsym (unversioned) on non-x86_64 — works but costs a wasted dlvsym call. Fix: per-arch version constants.

Discovered during bd-j946 review.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:08:36.457319974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:08:36.457319974Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-1iya", "title": "bd-rqn subtask: Compile-time profile gates enforcing production-only default kernel", "description": "Background:
- Classification alone is insufficient; feature/build boundaries must enforce it.

Goal:
- Implement compile-time gating so default production builds exclude research-only controllers by construction.

Deliverables:
1) Feature flag architecture for production vs research kernels.
2) Build profile checks ensuring expected module set per profile.
3) Guard scripts to detect accidental profile drift.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Default production build contains only approved production controllers.
- Research profile can opt-in additional controllers without affecting production path.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for profile/module mapping.
- CI checks across profile combinations.
- Structured logs for build-profile validation outcomes.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "Codex", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:02:38.464980345Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-12T22:34:07.345472796Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-12T22:33:36.904979185Z", "close_reason": "Implemented runtime_math profile-gate script + compile-time enforcement + harness mapping tests + CI extended-gate hook", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["build", "ci", "critique", "math", "verification"], "comments": [{"id": 677, "issue_id": "bd-1iya", "author": "Codex", "text": "Starting bd-1iya. Plan: (1) define Cargo feature/profile split for production vs research runtime_math modules (default=production), (2) generate/validate compile-time module set vs manifest+classification matrix, (3) add gate script + harness tests to prevent drift, (4) wire into scripts/ci.sh extended gates.", "created_at": "2026-02-12T22:24:22Z"}, {"id": 678, "issue_id": "bd-1iya", "author": "Codex", "text": "Landed bd-1iya in commit 4d3bd6c.

Changes:
- Enforced  at compile time (membrane compile_error if disabled)
- Added build-profile gate: scripts/check_runtime_math_profile_gates.sh (writes JSONL logs + report)
- Added harness mapping test: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/runtime_math_profile_gates_test.rs
- Wired gate into scripts/ci.sh extended gates

Validation:
- scripts/check_runtime_math_profile_gates.sh (PASS)
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_profile_gates_test (3 passed)
- cargo fmt --check (PASS)

Structured artifacts:
- target/conformance/runtime_math_profile_gates.log.jsonl
- target/conformance/runtime_math_profile_gates.report.json", "created_at": "2026-02-12T22:33:43Z"}, {"id": 679, "issue_id": "bd-1iya", "author": "Codex", "text": "Correction: enforced runtime-math-production at compile time (crate refuses to build with --no-default-features / without runtime-math-production).", "created_at": "2026-02-12T22:34:07Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-1j2u", "title": "bd-1gh subtask: pthread_key registry architecture and lifecycle semantics", "description": "Background:
- pthread_key APIs are currently blocking stub-burn-down progress and depend on thread lifecycle correctness.

Goal:
- Define and implement key-table architecture with deterministic key allocation/deallocation semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Global key registry design and limits policy.
2) Thread-local value slot mapping strategy.
3) key_create/key_delete semantics and error paths.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Key lifecycle semantics are deterministic and race-safe.
- Explicit handling for exhausted key space and invalid keys.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for key allocation/deletion and invalid operations.
- Structured logs: trace_id, key_id, op, result, errno, timing.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:00:52.416477429Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T17:34:32.794586803Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T17:34:32.794496324Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "implementation", "pthread", "testing", "tls"], "comments": [{"id": 680, "issue_id": "bd-1j2u", "author": "PurpleHawk", "text": "PurpleHawk: Key registry architecture verified and expanded with edge-case tests. Implementation in tls.rs is solid: Mutex-protected global key registry (1024 slots), open-addressed TID→values-ptr hash table (4096 slots, allocation-free), generation counter for stale key detection. Added 12 new tests: out-of-bounds keys, generation counter semantics, slot reuse with different destructors, double-teardown safety, table operations on nonexistent TIDs, 64-key independence, key_delete does not call destructors, zero-value handling. Total: 29 passing tests.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T17:34:31Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-1j4", "title": "EPIC: Hard Parts Roadmap (rtld/CRT + NSS/locale/resolver)", "description": "Critique mapping: #4.

Goal: make the ‘hard parts’ explicit with phased milestones.

Deliverables:
- Bootstrap strategy doc (what ‘drop-in replacement’ means for us).
- Incremental plan for crt startup, loader, NSS/resolv, locale/iconv.

Acceptance:
- Each missing subsystem has a bead with acceptance tests and a measurable milestone.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Success Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.", "notes": "## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.", "status": "open", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:36:07.020884278Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-11T04:28:03.314006841Z", "close_reason": "Hard Parts Roadmap: 5/9 children closed. RTLD phase-1, NSS/resolver, locale/iconv, hard-parts battery all complete. Remaining items are release-gate epics.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "locale", "nss", "resolver", "rtld"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 681, "issue_id": "bd-1j4", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T22:25:08Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-j946","title":"vDSO gettime fastpath: add parity + fallback coverage","description":"Review of cod's recent vDSO fastpath (commit 92989d2/time_abi.rs) found coverage gaps in tests: (1) vdso_fastpath_snapshot and hit-counter tests confirm the fastpath is HIT but never verify the fastpath AGREES with the syscall fallback — a silent divergence (e.g., wrong clock_id mapping) would pass the existing tests. (2) No test verifies that CLOCK_PROCESS_CPUTIME_ID (not vDSO-exported) does NOT increment vdso_clock_gettime_hits. (3) No test for EINVAL propagation when vdso_clock_gettime rejects an unsupported clock. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/time_abi_test.rs. Use raw_syscall::sys_clock_gettime as ground-truth reference to avoid libc-symbol interception issues.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:05:29.950988891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:05:35.953433160Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id": "bd-ju7", "title": "Drift: runtime_math module cadence classification (hot path vs cadence; strict vs hardened)", "description": "Create an explicit classification of every runtime_math module (existing + new) along these axes:

- Hot-path vs cadence:
  - `hot`: runs inside `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` strict fast path
  - `cadence`: runs only on epoch boundaries / resample points
- Mode surface:
  - used in strict
  - used only in hardened
- Data dependencies:
  - uses only integer/fixed-point state
  - uses floats (must be cadence-only if strict budget matters)
  - uses locks/mutex (must not be on strict hot path)

## Why This Matters
This is the “extreme optimization” guardrail: we can’t reason about perf without knowing which modules execute when.

## Deliverable
- A table (in a bead comment or short doc) listing each module and its classification.
- The table must include legacy anchors: which API families/subsystems the module protects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No module remains “unknown”; every module is classified.

Full cadence classification of all 45 runtime_math modules.

## Architecture Pattern: Deferred-Update

```
decide() [hot path, every call]
├─ AtomicU8/U64 reads (43 modules) — lock-free, integer only
├─ Direct method calls (2 modules: risk, cohomology) — lock-free
└─ Every 64th call: resample_high_order_kernels()
    └─ risk.evaluate() [lock-free], oracle.lock() [Mutex]

observe_validation_result() [cold path, after validation]
├─ 43 Mutex-protected modules updated sequentially
│  └─ Each state encoded to AtomicU8 and stored
└─ 25-element severity vector fed to fusion
   └─ 18 meta-controllers updated in second cascade
```

## Hot-Path Modules (2, both lock-free):
1. **risk** — `upper_bound_ppm()` direct call, conformal arithmetic, u32
2. **cohomology** — `fault_count()` direct call, u64 counter

## Cadence-Only Modules (43, behind Mutex in observe):
All follow the pattern: Mutex<Controller> updated in observe → state encoded to AtomicU8 → read lock-free in decide.

### AtomicU8 scalar (37 modules):
spectral, rough_path, persistence, bridge, hji, mfg, padic, symplectic, sparse, topos, audit, changepoint, conformal, loss_minimizer, coupling, microlocal, serre, clifford, ktheory, covering, tstructure, atiyah_bott, pomdp, sos, admm, obstruction, operator_norm, malliavin, info_geometry, matrix_concentration, nerve_complex, wasserstein, kernel_mmd, pac_bayes, stein, lyapunov, rademacher, transfer_entropy, hodge, provenance, grobner, grothendieck

### AtomicU8[COUNT] family-indexed (3 modules):
eprocess, cvar, large_deviations

### AtomicU64 multi-field (3 modules):
design (ident_ppm, budget_ns, expected_ns, selected), fusion (bonus_ppm, entropy_milli, drift_ppm, dominant_signal), equivariant (alignment_ppm, orbit)

## Mode Gating: NONE
All 45 modules run in BOTH strict and hardened modes. Mode only affects action selection (Allow vs Repair), not controller activation.

## Barrier Oracle:
Called lock-free in decide() at line 1253 — `barrier.admissible()`. Read-only guard, no observation needed. Classified as **hot/read-only**.

## Design Subsystem:
Special case — Mutex acquired in decide() but ONLY every 256th call (line ~1157). Cadence-gated.

## Summary:
- 0 modules with forbidden ops on strict hot path (no exp/ln/matrix)
- All float computation deferred to observe phase behind Mutex
- decide() hot path is 100% integer/atomic reads + 2 lock-free calls
- Strict overhead dominated by bonus summation (~40 atomic loads)", "created_at": "2026-02-10T07:18:31Z"}]}

+/// Typed `io_uring_setup(entries, params)` helper over `struct io_uring_params`.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `params` must point to writable `IoUringParams` storage.
+#[inline]
+#[allow(unsafe_code)]
+pub unsafe fn sys_io_uring_setup_params(
+    entries: u32,
+    params: *mut IoUringParams,
+) -> Result<i32, i32> {
+    unsafe { sys_io_uring_setup(entries, params.cast::<u8>()) }
+}
